description: Hand-curated anchor compounds for MCAS/MCAD hypothesis generation. The build script (scripts/build_compound_library.py) reads this file and auto-fetches canonical SMILES from PubChem PUG-REST by CID. Entries flagged as 'biologic' or 'peptide' or 'extract' have no small-molecule SMILES and are handled separately downstream.

evidence_levels.high: human clinical / approved drug / strong human data
evidence_levels.medium: in vitro on human mast cells (LAD2 / HMC-1) + animal + anecdotal MCAS reports
evidence_levels.low: mechanistic / preclinical / extrapolation from related conditions

compounds.name: Cetirizine
compounds.mechanism: Selective peripheral H1 receptor antagonist
compounds.evidence_notes: First-line MCAS rescue; reduces histamine-driven urticaria, pruritus, rhinorrhea.
source_refs: Afrin LB et al. 2020 MCAS consensus-2

compounds.name: Loratadine
compounds.mechanism: Selective peripheral H1 receptor antagonist
compounds.evidence_notes: Non-sedating alternative; common in MCAS step-1 protocols.

compounds.name: Fexofenadine
compounds.mechanism: Selective peripheral H1 receptor antagonist
compounds.evidence_notes: Often used at 2-4x standard dose in MCAS per consensus. Large (502 Da), zwitterionic, P-glycoprotein substrate — by design peripherally-restricted. Oral only; buccal/sublingual route NOT expected to give fast onset.
compounds.route_notes: Crushing + buccal use would NOT replicate the fast onset of first-gen H1s like diphenhydramine — opposite chemistry. See docs/route-of-administration.md.

compounds.name: Hydroxyzine
compounds.mechanism: H1 antagonist + anxiolytic; crosses BBB
compounds.evidence_notes: Used for sleep + neuro symptoms in MCAS.

compounds.name: Diphenhydramine
compounds.mechanism: H1 antagonist; rescue route for anaphylaxis
compounds.evidence_notes: Acute rescue; sedating. Small (255 Da), lipophilic (logP 3.3), single tertiary amine — chemistry strongly favors buccal / sublingual / IM rescue with fast onset (5-15 min).
compounds.route_notes: Patient-reported fast buccal rescue (crushed + held in cheek) is consistent with the physicochemistry. See docs/route-of-administration.md.

compounds.name: Famotidine
compounds.mechanism: H2 receptor antagonist
compounds.evidence_notes: Standard with H1 in MCAS; GI + systemic histamine.

compounds.name: Ranitidine
compounds.mechanism: H2 receptor antagonist
compounds.evidence_notes: Withdrawn 2020 (NDMA impurity). Included for SAR/reference only.

compounds.name: Cromolyn sodium
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits mast cell degranulation; reduces Ca2+ influx
compounds.evidence_notes: Oral (Gastrocrom) for GI MCAS; poorly absorbed systemically. Disodium salt makes it incompatible with buccal absorption.
compounds.route_notes: Oral cromolyn works *locally* on GI mast cells, not via systemic absorption — that's the design intent. Inhaled / nasal / eye formulations target their respective mucosae.

compounds.name: Ketotifen
compounds.mechanism: H1 antagonist + mast cell stabilizer
compounds.evidence_notes: Off-label in US; widely used by MCAS clinicians.

compounds.name: Ascorbic acid
compounds.mechanism: Accelerates histamine degradation; antioxidant; cofactor for DAO
compounds.evidence_notes: Adjunct; supports DAO activity.

compounds.name: Quercetin
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits PKC, Ca2+ influx, histamine + tryptase + cytokine release
compounds.evidence_notes: Outperforms cromolyn in human mast cell assays for some readouts.
source_refs: Weng Z et al. 2012 PLOS ONE; Theoharides 2015

compounds.name: Bromelain
compounds.mechanism: Reduces allergic inflammation; possible histamine modulation
compounds.evidence_notes: Adjunct in allergic rhinitis; mechanism not fully resolved.

compounds.name: Stinging nettle leaf
compounds.mechanism: Natural H1 antagonism + COX/LOX inhibition; multi-component
compounds.evidence_notes: Standardized extracts (Urtica dioica) studied in allergic rhinitis.

compounds.name: Diamine oxidase (DAO)
compounds.mechanism: Degrades dietary histamine in the gut lumen pre-absorption
compounds.evidence_notes: Taken before high-histamine meals; useful in histamine intolerance subset.

compounds.name: Montelukast
compounds.mechanism: CysLT1 receptor antagonist; blocks LTD4/LTE4
compounds.evidence_notes: Standard MCAS maintenance; respiratory + GI symptoms.

compounds.name: Naltrexone (low-dose)
compounds.mechanism: TLR4 antagonism; glial / immune modulation at low dose
compounds.evidence_notes: Anecdotal in MCAS, fibromyalgia, autoimmune; off-label.

compounds.name: Luteolin
compounds.mechanism: Inhibits PKC, NF-kB, mast cell + microglia activation; crosses BBB
compounds.evidence_notes: Stronger than quercetin in several neuro-mast-cell assays.

compounds.name: Curcumin
compounds.mechanism: NF-kB inhibition; Nrf2 activation; antioxidant
compounds.evidence_notes: Phytosomal forms (Meriva) improve bioavailability.

compounds.name: EGCG
compounds.mechanism: Mast cell stabilization; antioxidant; Nrf2 activator
compounds.evidence_notes: Polyphenol with broad anti-allergic effects.

compounds.name: Resveratrol
compounds.mechanism: Sirtuin / Nrf2 activator; MRGPRX2 inhibition
compounds.evidence_notes: Inhibits non-IgE pseudo-allergic activation.

compounds.name: Silibinin
compounds.mechanism: Hepatoprotective; antioxidant; mast cell modulation
compounds.evidence_notes: Active component of silymarin complex.

compounds.name: Apigenin
compounds.mechanism: Mast cell stabilizer; NF-kB inhibition
compounds.evidence_notes: Synergizes with quercetin/luteolin.

compounds.name: Kaempferol
compounds.mechanism: Antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; mast cell modulation
compounds.evidence_notes: Common dietary flavonoid; broad signaling effects.

compounds.name: Hesperidin
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; capillary stabilization
compounds.evidence_notes: Studied in allergic rhinitis.

compounds.name: Rutin
compounds.mechanism: Antioxidant; histamine release inhibition
compounds.evidence_notes: Quercetin-3-rutinoside; lower bioavailability.

compounds.name: Myricitrin
compounds.mechanism: Antioxidant; anti-allergic
compounds.evidence_notes: Less-studied flavonoid worth SAR exploration.

compounds.name: Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3)
compounds.mechanism: Immunomodulation; mast cell stability; T-reg support
compounds.evidence_notes: Deficiency common in MCAS; correction reduces flare frequency.

compounds.name: Calcitriol
compounds.mechanism: VDR agonist; downregulates mast cell IgE response
compounds.evidence_notes: Active hormone; clinical use mainly in renal disease.

compounds.name: Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA)
compounds.mechanism: SPM precursor; anti-inflammatory; membrane modulation
compounds.evidence_notes: Reduces systemic inflammation; supports membrane stability.

compounds.name: Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA)
compounds.mechanism: SPM precursor; reduces LTB4; mast cell modulation
compounds.evidence_notes: Co-administered with DHA in fish oil.

compounds.name: Butyric acid
compounds.mechanism: HDAC inhibition; gut barrier reinforcement; Treg induction
compounds.evidence_notes: Microbiome-derived; supports gut-mast axis.

compounds.name: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC)
compounds.mechanism: GSH replenishment; ROS scavenging; Nrf2 activation (indirect)
compounds.evidence_notes: Supports oxidative-stress reduction upstream.

compounds.name: Taurine
compounds.mechanism: Membrane stabilization; osmoregulation; anti-inflammatory
compounds.evidence_notes: Down-regulates degranulation in some assays.

compounds.name: Alpha-lipoic acid
compounds.mechanism: Recycles GSH/Vit C/E; Nrf2 activator
compounds.evidence_notes: Neuroprotective; mitochondrial support.

compounds.name: Biotin
compounds.mechanism: Cofactor for carboxylases; downregulates degranulation (animal data)
compounds.evidence_notes: Adjunct only; weak direct evidence.

compounds.name: Baicalein
compounds.mechanism: Potent mast cell stabilizer; 12-LOX inhibitor
compounds.evidence_notes: Strong inhibition of histamine + leukotriene release.

compounds.name: Rosmarinic acid
compounds.mechanism: Anti-allergic; mast cell stabilization
compounds.evidence_notes: Studied in seasonal allergy trials.

compounds.name: Thymoquinone
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; histamine pathway inhibition
compounds.evidence_notes: Active component of black cumin seed oil.

compounds.name: 6-Gingerol
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; prostaglandin inhibition; mast cell stability
compounds.evidence_notes: Common GI-supportive natural.

compounds.name: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid)
compounds.mechanism: COX-1/2 inhibition; PGD2 suppression
compounds.evidence_notes: Used in prostaglandin-high MCAS subset under clinical supervision (NSAID-tolerant only).

compounds.name: Masitinib
compounds.mechanism: KIT, LYN, FYN inhibition
compounds.evidence_notes: Phase 3 trials for severe indolent systemic mastocytosis / severe MCAS.

compounds.name: Sulforaphane
compounds.mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier (Cys-151/273/288 isothiocyanate adduct) -> Nrf2 stabilization + nuclear translocation; HO-1 / NQO1 induction; NF-kB / caspase-1 inhibition; epigenetic (DNMT/HDAC) modulation
compounds.evidence_notes: Upstream rescue candidate for oxidative + epigenetic 'injury'; suppresses mast cell degranulation in vitro. Small (177 Da), modestly lipophilic — absorbs sublingually from chewed broccoli sprouts (myrosinase activation in mouth).
compounds.route_notes: Chewed broccoli sprouts is a natural sublingual / oral route — chewing activates myrosinase which converts glucoraphanin to SFN in the mouth before swallow.

compounds.name: Glucoraphanin
compounds.mechanism: Converted to sulforaphane by myrosinase; same downstream Nrf2 activation
compounds.evidence_notes: Bioavailability depends on co-administered myrosinase or gut bacteria.

compounds.name: Semaglutide
compounds.mechanism: GLP-1R agonism; broad anti-inflammatory; possible direct mast cell stabilization
compounds.evidence_notes: 2025 refractory-MCAS case series: ~89% response. Peptide — no canonical small-molecule SMILES.

compounds.name: Tirzepatide
compounds.mechanism: Dual GLP-1R + GIPR agonism; anti-inflammatory; metabolic stabilization
compounds.evidence_notes: Same 2025 case series cohort. Peptide.

compounds.name: Omalizumab
compounds.mechanism: Binds free IgE; downregulates FcεRI surface expression on mast cells/basophils
compounds.evidence_notes: Approved for chronic spontaneous urticaria; off-label in MCAS. Antibody — no SMILES.

compounds.name: Avapritinib
compounds.mechanism: Selective KIT D816V tyrosine kinase inhibition
compounds.evidence_notes: FDA-approved for advanced systemic mastocytosis (2021).

compounds.name: Iberin
compounds.mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier (Cys); Nrf2 activator; shorter-chain SFN analog
compounds.evidence_notes: Found in cabbage, broccoli. Same warhead as SFN; one CH2 shorter.

compounds.name: Erucin
compounds.mechanism: Sulfide form of SFN; reduced + bioequivalent in vivo via S-oxidation; covalent KEAP1 modifier
compounds.evidence_notes: Found in arugula / Eruca sativa. Plasma half-life longer than SFN; interconverts in vivo.

compounds.name: Sulforaphene
compounds.mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier; unsaturated SFN homolog with potentially stronger Nrf2 activation
compounds.evidence_notes: From radish seeds (Raphanus sativus). C2=C3 double bond vs SFN.

compounds.name: Allyl isothiocyanate
compounds.mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier; TRPA1 agonist (pungent)
compounds.evidence_notes: From mustard / horseradish / wasabi. Smaller and more volatile than SFN.

compounds.name: Benzyl isothiocyanate
compounds.mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier; broader cytotoxic profile
compounds.evidence_notes: From garden cress, papaya seeds. Aromatic distal anchor; explores another pharmacophore class.

compounds.name: Phenethyl isothiocyanate
compounds.mechanism: Covalent KEAP1 modifier; HDAC inhibition; well-studied chemopreventive
compounds.evidence_notes: From watercress (Nasturtium officinale). Strong Nrf2 + epigenetic activity.

compounds.name: Midostaurin
compounds.mechanism: KIT, FLT3, PKC inhibition
compounds.evidence_notes: Approved for advanced systemic mastocytosis.

compounds.name: Cannabidiol
compounds.mechanism: CB2 weak agonist + TRPV1/TRPA1 modulator + 5-HT1A partial agonist; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; PPAR-gamma; some Nrf2 induction
compounds.evidence_notes: Multiple in vitro studies show inhibition of mast-cell degranulation; clinical anti-inflammatory data in epilepsy + atopic conditions. CBD-rich extracts anecdotally reported in MCAS communities.

compounds.name: Cannabigerol
compounds.mechanism: CB1/CB2 weak partial agonist; alpha-2 adrenergic agonist; 5-HT1A antagonist; anti-inflammatory
compounds.evidence_notes: Parent cannabinoid; less mast-cell data than CBD but anti-inflammatory profile + emerging interest in IBD/skin conditions.

compounds.name: Cannabichromene
compounds.mechanism: TRPA1 agonist; endocannabinoid-system modulator; anti-inflammatory
compounds.evidence_notes: Less-studied minor cannabinoid; preclinical anti-inflammatory + analgesic effects.

compounds.name: Cannabinol
compounds.mechanism: CB2-biased partial agonist; sedative
compounds.evidence_notes: Forms as THC oxidizes; CB2-biased profile of interest for peripheral immune modulation.

compounds.name: Delta-9-THC
compounds.subcategory: Phytocannabinoid (intoxicating; controlled substance, US Schedule I as of pre-2024 status)
compounds.mechanism: CB1/CB2 partial agonist; mast-cell-stabilizing in vitro via CB2
compounds.evidence_notes: Bisogno 1997 / Facci 1995 showed THC inhibits mast-cell degranulation in vitro. Regulatory caveat: Schedule I in US; DEA rescheduling under review. Listed for in-silico scoring only.

compounds.name: Delta-8-THC
compounds.subcategory: Phytocannabinoid (intoxicating but less potent than delta-9)
compounds.mechanism: CB1/CB2 agonist; biased toward CB2 vs delta-9
compounds.evidence_notes: Position isomer of delta-9-THC. Federal hemp-derived legal status in US under 2018 Farm Bill; rapidly evolving.

compounds.name: Tetrahydrocannabivarin
compounds.mechanism: CB1 neutral antagonist + CB2 partial agonist (dose-dependent)
compounds.evidence_notes: Propyl side chain in place of THC's pentyl. Anti-inflammatory + appetite suppressant profile in preclinical work.

compounds.name: Cannabidivarin
compounds.mechanism: TRPA1/TRPV1 modulator; CB1/CB2 weak affinity; anti-inflammatory
compounds.evidence_notes: Non-intoxicating CBD analog. Some seizure trials. Less mast-cell data than CBD.

compounds.name: Palmitoylethanolamide
compounds.mechanism: PPAR-alpha agonist + indirect endocannabinoid 'entourage' (raises AEA tone) + direct mast-cell stabilization (ALIA pathway, Levi-Montalcini coinage)
compounds.evidence_notes: Strongest non-rx natural-product mast-cell-stabilizer evidence base. ~30 clinical trials in pain, atopy, IBD. Used as ultra-micronized formulation (Normast, PeaPure). Safe; no abuse potential.

compounds.name: Oleoylethanolamide
compounds.mechanism: PPAR-alpha agonist; anti-inflammatory + satiety signal
compounds.evidence_notes: PEA's mono-unsaturated congener. Less direct mast-cell data; included for class completeness.

compounds.name: Anandamide
compounds.mechanism: CB1/CB2 partial agonist + TRPV1; endogenous endocannabinoid
compounds.evidence_notes: Endogenous; pharmacological supplementation is impractical (rapid FAAH degradation). Scored for class context; therapeutic targeting is via FAAH inhibitors (not in library).

compounds.name: 2-Arachidonoylglycerol
compounds.mechanism: CB1/CB2 full agonist; major endocannabinoid signal
compounds.evidence_notes: Endogenous; rapid MAGL degradation; supplementation impractical. Class anchor.

compounds.name: Beta-caryophyllene
compounds.mechanism: Selective CB2 agonist (Ki ~155 nM) — unique among dietary terpenes; mast-cell stabilizer; anti-inflammatory in colitis/arthritis models
compounds.evidence_notes: Found in black pepper, cloves, hops, cannabis. FDA GRAS food additive — only CB2 agonist with that status. Strongest single-terpene case for MCAS-axis activity.

compounds.name: Alpha-humulene
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory (TNF-alpha / IL-1beta suppression); analgesic
compounds.evidence_notes: Co-occurs with beta-caryophyllene; weaker CB2 affinity than caryophyllene but anti-inflammatory in airway + colitis models.

compounds.name: Alpha-pinene
compounds.mechanism: Bronchodilator; acetylcholinesterase inhibitor (mild); anti-inflammatory
compounds.evidence_notes: Pine, rosemary, conifers. Bronchodilator profile of interest for respiratory MCAS phenotype. Also a documented MCAS VOC trigger for some individuals — bidirectional.

compounds.name: Beta-pinene
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; anti-microbial; bronchodilator
compounds.evidence_notes: Position isomer of alpha-pinene. Similar bidirectional VOC behavior in MCAS.

compounds.name: D-Limonene
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; GSH induction; mast-cell stabilization (LAD2 in vitro)
compounds.evidence_notes: Citrus peel oil. Hirota 2010 showed inhibition of mast-cell-mediated allergic inflammation. Also a top-tier MCAS VOC trigger in fragrance reports — strongly bidirectional.

compounds.name: Linalool
compounds.mechanism: Anxiolytic + anti-inflammatory + mast-cell stabilizer in vitro
compounds.evidence_notes: Lavender, basil, coriander. Kim 1999 showed inhibition of compound 48/80 mast-cell activation. Also documented MCAS fragrance trigger.

compounds.name: Beta-myrcene
compounds.mechanism: Sedative; analgesic; anti-inflammatory; weak mu-opioid potentiator
compounds.evidence_notes: Hops, cannabis, mango. Sedative + muscle-relaxant profile.

compounds.name: Eucalyptol
compounds.mechanism: Mucolytic; anti-inflammatory (NF-kB suppression); bronchodilator
compounds.evidence_notes: Eucalyptus, rosemary, tea tree. Approved as inhaled mucolytic in EU (Soledum). Bidirectional: also respiratory trigger for some individuals.

compounds.name: Terpinolene
compounds.mechanism: Antioxidant; sedative; mild anti-inflammatory
compounds.evidence_notes: Cannabis, tea tree, nutmeg. Less-studied terpene; included for class breadth.

compounds.name: Geraniol
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro
compounds.evidence_notes: Rose, palmarosa, citronella. EU fragrance allergen 26 — common contact-allergic trigger. Bidirectional in MCAS context.

compounds.name: Alpha-bisabolol
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; mast-cell stabilizer in vitro; gastric-mucosa protective
compounds.evidence_notes: Roman + German chamomile. Rare trigger; mostly anti-inflammatory profile.

compounds.name: Nerolidol
compounds.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; transdermal penetration enhancer; anti-microbial
compounds.evidence_notes: Neroli, jasmine, lemongrass. Skin-permeation enhancer property is double-edged.
